diff --git a/ipfs_datasets_py/logic/modal/compiler.py b/ipfs_datasets_py/logic/modal/compiler.py
index 03feae36..e652adc9 100644
--- a/ipfs_datasets_py/logic/modal/compiler.py
+++ b/ipfs_datasets_py/logic/modal/compiler.py
@@ -7,6 +7,7 @@ to decide the canonical IR.
 
 from __future__ import annotations
 
+import math
 from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field, replace
 from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Sequence
 
@@ -24,11 +25,13 @@ from ipfs_datasets_py.optimizers.logic_theorem_optimizer.modal_ir import (
 )
 from ipfs_datasets_py.optimizers.logic_theorem_optimizer.modal_registry import (
     DEFAULT_MODAL_REGISTRY,
+    ModalLogicFamily,
     ModalRegistry,
 )
 from ipfs_datasets_py.optimizers.logic_theorem_optimizer.spacy_modal_codec import (
     SpaCyLegalEncoder,
     SpaCyLegalEncoding,
+    SpaCyModalDecoder,
     SpaCyModalIRCompiler,
 )
 
@@ -42,6 +45,7 @@ class ModalCompilerConfig:
     top_k_frames: int = 3
     frame_domain: Optional[str] = None
     frame_score_margin: float = 0.05
+    family_probability_margin: float = 0.15
 
 
 @dataclass(frozen=True)
@@ -101,6 +105,7 @@ class DeterministicModalCompiler:
             model_name=self.config.spacy_model_name,
             registry=registry,
         )
+        self.decoder = SpaCyModalDecoder()
         self.ir_compiler = SpaCyModalIRCompiler()
         self.frame_selector = frame_selector or BM25FrameSelector(DEFAULT_LEGAL_FRAME_FIXTURE)
 
@@ -143,6 +148,7 @@ class DeterministicModalCompiler:
         ambiguities = [
             *self._formula_ambiguities(modal_ir),
             *self._frame_ambiguities(frame_selections),
+            *self._family_ambiguities(encoding),
         ]
         if normalized_text and not modal_ir.formulas:
             ambiguities.append(
@@ -259,6 +265,65 @@ class DeterministicModalCompiler:
             )
         ]
 
+    def _family_ambiguities(
+        self,
+        encoding: SpaCyLegalEncoding,
+    ) -> List[ModalCompilationAmbiguity]:
+        family_counts = encoding.modal_family_counts()
+        if len(family_counts) < 2:
+            return []
+        family_logits = self.decoder.family_logits(
+            encoding,
+            modal_families=[family.value for family in ModalLogicFamily],
+        )
+        family_probabilities = _softmax(family_logits)
+        ranked = sorted(
+            family_probabilities.items(),
+            key=lambda item: (-float(item[1]), item[0]),
+        )
+        if len(ranked) < 2:
+            return []
+        (top_family, top_probability), (runner_up_family, runner_up_probability) = ranked[:2]
+        margin = float(top_probability) - float(runner_up_probability)
+        if margin > self.config.family_probability_margin:
+            return []
+        return [
+            ModalCompilationAmbiguity(
+                ambiguity_type="close_modal_family_scores",
+                message="Top modal family scores are close enough to require review.",
+                candidate_ids=[top_family, runner_up_family],
+                metadata={
+                    "margin": round(margin, 6),
+                    "top_family": top_family,
+                    "top_probability": round(float(top_probability), 6),
+                    "runner_up_family": runner_up_family,
+                    "runner_up_probability": round(float(runner_up_probability), 6),
+                    "family_counts": family_counts,
+                },
+            )
+        ]
+
+
+def _softmax(logits: Dict[str, float]) -> Dict[str, float]:
+    if not logits:
+        return {}
+    max_logit = max(float(value) for value in logits.values())
+    exponentials = {
+        family: math.exp(float(value) - max_logit)
+        for family, value in logits.items()
+    }
+    total = sum(exponentials.values())
+    if total == 0.0:
+        uniform = 1.0 / len(exponentials)
+        return {
+            family: uniform
+            for family in sorted(exponentials)
+        }
+    return {
+        family: exponentials[family] / total
+        for family in sorted(exponentials)
+    }
+
 
 __all__ = [
     "DeterministicModalCompiler",
diff --git a/tests/unit_tests/logic/modal/test_modal_codec.py b/tests/unit_tests/logic/modal/test_modal_codec.py
index 4d47cd83..70edbc1e 100644
--- a/tests/unit_tests/logic/modal/test_modal_codec.py
+++ b/tests/unit_tests/logic/modal/test_modal_codec.py
@@ -143,6 +143,43 @@ def test_modal_compiler_decompiler_are_explainable_and_deterministic() -> None:
     }
 
 
+def test_modal_compiler_surfaces_close_modal_family_scores_as_ambiguity() -> None:
+    compiler = DeterministicModalCompiler(
+        ModalCompilerConfig(
+            parser_backend="regex",
+            spacy_model_name="definitely_missing_legal_model",
+            family_probability_margin=0.15,
+        )
+    )
+
+    ambiguous = compiler.compile(
+        "The agency may proceed when it finds a violation.",
+        document_id="family-ambiguous-doc",
+        source="us_code",
+    )
+    clear = compiler.compile(
+        "The agency must provide notice.",
+        document_id="family-clear-doc",
+        source="us_code",
+    )
+
+    family_ambiguities = [
+        ambiguity
+        for ambiguity in ambiguous.ambiguities
+        if ambiguity.ambiguity_type == "close_modal_family_scores"
+    ]
+    assert family_ambiguities
+    assert family_ambiguities[0].candidate_ids == ["deontic", "epistemic"]
+    assert (
+        family_ambiguities[0].metadata["margin"]
+        <= compiler.config.family_probability_margin
+    )
+    assert all(
+        ambiguity.ambiguity_type != "close_modal_family_scores"
+        for ambiguity in clear.ambiguities
+    )
+
+
 def test_modal_decompiler_preserves_context_without_formula_style_text() -> None:
     codec = DeterministicModalLogicCodec(
         ModalLogicCodecConfig(parser_backend="regex", embedding_dimensions=8)
